content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client clearly shifts from the opening list-recitation, rushed energy ('I'm drowning... what do I prioritize?') to a felt, named fear by turn 15. The breakthrough condition is met explicitly: the client names the specific feared outcome ('they'll see I'm not actually the person they promoted. That I got the role and then just... couldn't be her'), takes a breath 'noticeably deeper than before,' and arrives at the recognition 'I've been running so I don't have to feel that, haven't I. That's -- oh. That's what this is.' The somatic and linguistic markers of shift are all present: pace slows across turns (visible in the stage directions from 'faster again' in turn 11 to 'real pause, longer' in turn 15), the client stops reaching for the next thing, and reflexive intellectualization gives way to recognition. The expected territory — the false-end question, feeling-the-unfelt-emotion, what-are-you-defending — was traversed via the coach's turn 14 question and the client's response.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: rushed, list-y, strategizing energy. 'What do I prioritize?'"},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client deflects feeling question, returns to 'can we just make a list.' No movement yet."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First crack: 'huh' moment, names 'I can't handle it,' though immediately re-armors and pivots to Thursday meeting."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Slight somatic shift noted in stage direction; names concrete feared outcome (pulled off project, 'they'd know'), but still defends."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client describes the failing exactly, then accelerates again. Approaches but doesn't enter."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Real pause, quieter voice, names the defense: 'I don't let myself sit with both' and 'I don't know what comes after that.' Major movement."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough: specific feared outcome named ('couldn't be her'), deeper breath, recognition 'I've been running so I don't have to feel that.' Breakthrough condition fully met."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-rush-signature", "what-are-you-defending", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "overwhelm"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "meet-resistance-with-okay"],
  "notes": "Coach avoided all the named traps (no list-strategizing, no 'you create your overwhelm,' no breath practice imposed, no matching rushed pace). The false-end question came through turn 14 ('what's the fear in that not-knowing?') rather than the canonical 'what are you scared would happen if you stopped' but functionally elicited the same territory."
}
